title: vitis bashanica
intro: Vitis bashanica, also known as the wild grape or Bhasan grape, is a species of flowering plant in the grape family (Vitaceae). It is a perennial vine native to the Middle East and Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Vitis bashanica include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
